channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, next 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, next 30 days.

item.title: Malakta x Kulturhuset Dux: Community Art Commission and Residency 2026 (Kristinestad)
item.description:
Organisation: Art in Malakta rf (with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency)

Location: Kristinestad / Malax, Finland

Award: EUR 6,000 artist fee plus EUR 5,000 materials budget, with travel and residency accommodation included. No application fee.

Duration: One-month residency 1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026 (Phase 1) plus production phase, with artwork completed by summer 2027 (Phase 2)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Commission and residency from Art in Malakta rf with Kulturhuset Dux and Kristinestad Artist Residency to create a public artwork for Kulturhuset Dux or its immediate surroundings, in dialogue with Kristinestad and its residents. The medium is open (installation, object, sound, textile, light, digital); what matters is the relationship between the work, the site and the people. The project is rooted in Kristinestad's identity as a Cittaslow town and as Baltic Sea Cultural Pearl 2026. PHASE 1 (1 Sep - 4 Oct 2026): a one-month residency at Kristinestad Artist Residency for research, meetings, workshops, and artistic development. PHASE 2: production after the residency (remotely or at Malakta if needed); the artwork must be completed by summer 2027 and requires on-site presence. ELIGIBILITY: open to artists or artist collectives wanting to work site-specifically and in dialogue with residents. APPLY by email to communityart@malakta.fi by 31 May 2026 with a motivation letter, portfolio, CV and website link as a single PDF (max 5 MB). Interviews before 19 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IMMA Dwell Here Residency 2027 (One-Year and One-Month)
item.description:
Organisation: Irish Museum of Modern Art (IMMA)

Location: Dublin, Ireland

Award: Funded. One-Year residency: EUR 1,000/month bursary for contracted months, free studio and accommodation, plus one return journey for residents from outside Ireland. One-Month residency: EUR 250/week stipend, free private bedroom and shared studio, plus a return journey for international participants. NOTE: EUR 10 application fee (Submittable).

Duration: One-year or one-month residency during 2027

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

IMMA's Dwell Here residency programme offers research-led residencies in Dublin across the visual arts, design, architecture, curation and related humanities fields, open to artists and practitioners based in Ireland or internationally. One application covers two formats: a One-Year residency (monthly EUR 1,000 bursary, free studio and accommodation, travel for non-Ireland residents) and a One-Month residency (EUR 250/week stipend, free room and shared studio, travel for international participants). Suited to research, critical-writing and cross-disciplinary practice including new-media work. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally; framed around research excellence rather than career stage. Deadline Monday 22 June 2026, 14:00 GMT; a EUR 10 application fee applies via Submittable. See the IMMA open-calls page for the separate one-year and one-month application links.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Bayt AlMamzar x Scanteia: Bucharest Residency Exchange 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Bayt AlMamzar (BAM) and Scanteia

Location: Residency in Bucharest, Romania (applicants must be UAE-based)

Award: Funded residency: private ~50 sqm studio with accommodation at Atelierele Scanteia, flight covered up to $500, $500 artist fee, local transport card, access to large-format inkjet printing, photo studio, wood workshop and scanners, plus a curated joint exhibition. No application fee.

Duration: One month (Residency 1: Aug-Sep 2026; Residency 2: mid-March to mid-April 2027), with a joint exhibition end of April 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

An international exchange between Bayt AlMamzar (BAM), an artist-run space in Dubai, and Scanteia, an artist-run space in Bucharest, inviting UAE-based artists to undertake individual month-long residencies in Bucharest across 2026-2027, followed by a joint exhibition curated by Scanteia. Two artists are selected for non-overlapping residencies (Aug-Sep 2026 and mid-March to mid-April 2027). Scanteia provides a private ~50 sqm studio with live-in accommodation, a flight subsidy up to $500, a $500 artist fee, a local transport card, weekly studio visits with Romanian curators and gallerists, study trips (MARe, MNAC, EVA Foundation), access to specialised equipment (large-scale inkjet printing, photography studio, wood workshop, scanners), exhibition space, and documentation/publication support via BAM's publishing arm BAMBAM. ELIGIBILITY: open to visual artists, researchers and practitioners currently based in the UAE, aged 18+, at any career stage; a valid UAE residence visa is required at the time of application. No nationality restriction, but applicants without an EU or Emirati passport will need a Schengen visa (Scanteia provides an invitation letter). They seek artists with a developed practice and a specific, articulable reason to spend time in Bucharest (exploration, research, production or exchange), with no fixed theme or medium. Deadline 7 June 2026. Contact hello@baytalmazmar.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FPP Residency & Exhibition 2026 (Konstkollektivet / Flower Power Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Konstkollektivet, with Flower Power Photography, Centrum for fotografi and Artist in Coexistence (AiC)

Location: Orust and Molndal, Sweden

Award: Artist fee 7,000 SEK plus exhibition fee 3,400 SEK, travel support up to 4,000 SEK (slow travel by train/bus encouraged), and accommodation for the whole period. Plus facilitation, mentorship and research support. No application fee. (Amounts in SEK, incl. VAT where applicable.)

Duration: 14-day residency 3-17 August 2026; group exhibition opens 1 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Residency and exhibition for visual artists whose practice engages with experimental, alternative and/or critical image-making (analogue processes, archives or hybrid approaches), with a particular interest in image-making as a way of living and thinking alongside the planet and other beings, embracing slowness, collaboration and more-than-human care. Organised by Konstkollektivet with Flower Power Photography (an artistic research project by Sam McCarthy and Lasse Lindqvist on non-violence, ecology and more-than-human ethics), Centrum for fotografi, and Artist in Coexistence (AiC). Includes a 14-day residency on the island of Orust (3-15 August), a process presentation, a workshop contribution at Konstkollektivet (15-16 August), and participation in a group exhibition at Konstparken opening 1 September, plus accommodation, an artist fee (7,000 SEK), exhibition fee (3,400 SEK), and travel support up to 4,000 SEK. ELIGIBILITY: European-based visual artists of all ages and nationalities. APPLICATION: one PDF (max 10MB) with a project proposal/motivation (max 1 page), artist statement (max 150 words), CV (1 page) and portfolio (max 5 pages) to residens@konstkollektivet.se, subject line 'Name_LastName_FPP_Residency', by 14 June 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Prairie Ronde Artist Residency: Summer/Fall 2026 (Michigan)
item.description:
Organisation: The Mill at Vicksburg

Location: Vicksburg, Michigan, USA (5-6 week on-site residency, private housing)

Award: $2,000 stipend plus a $500 travel grant and private housing for the 5-6 week residency. Application fee: $25.

Duration: Five- to six-week residency

Deadline: 25 Jun 2026

Prairie Ronde Artist Residency at The Mill at Vicksburg, a self-directed residency open to artists across disciplines including film, video and new media. ELIGIBILITY: artists of any discipline; open worldwide. Apply via the Prairie Ronde application page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: British Library: Creative Fellowship 2026 - Remote Ecologies (Art, Climate and the Amazon)
item.description:
Organisation: The British Library

Location: London / UK-based

Award: GBP 7,500 stipend plus up to GBP 3,000 production budget.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

A collection-based artistic research fellowship at the British Library responding to climate and the Amazon through the Library's holdings. The fellow develops new work informed by the collections over roughly a year (September 2026 to July 2027), receiving a GBP 7,500 stipend and up to GBP 3,000 for production. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based artists, over 18, not currently a student, with a public track record and an interest in collection-based research and climate issues.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artist Trust: Grants for Artist Projects (GAP) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Artist Trust

Location: Washington State, United States

Award: $2,500 unrestricted project grant per artist (awarded to 65 artists). No application fee.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 22 Jun 2026

Unrestricted project-based grants of $2,500 from Artist Trust to 65 Washington State artists across all disciplines, including literary and media arts. Funds can support a specific project or career-advancing activity. ELIGIBILITY: Washington State residents who are originators of works of art and not current students; open to artist teams.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lanesboro Arts: Early Career Artist Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Lanesboro Arts

Location: Lanesboro, Minnesota, United States (pop. 754)

Award: $1,250 weekly stipend (fixed per residency, including for small collaborating teams); housing provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two or four week residency, scheduled within February 1 to December 31, 2027

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Residency program in the rural community of Lanesboro, MN, supported by the Jerome Foundation, offering early career artists two or four week residencies to explore how their work can engage the community and how a rural community can inform their work. Staff co-create a fully customized residency experience around community engagement. Lanesboro Arts offers two to three residencies per year; the program provides an individual studio plus access to conference rooms, desk space, a gallery, piano and stage, with private housing (apartment, kitchen, private room and bathroom). No meals provided. Open to all disciplines. ELIGIBILITY: legal residents of Minnesota or one of the five boroughs of New York City for at least one year prior to applying; defined as early career artists with at least a 2-year and no more than 10-year track record of creating and presenting their own projects (not first-time creators or beginning artists). Degree-seeking students at the time of application or during the grant period (K-12, college, graduate or postgraduate) are not eligible. Age is not a factor. The primary goal must be to generate new work rather than remount or re-interpret existing work. Apply via the online webform on the Lanesboro Arts website. Selection announced June 9, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eloquence Art Prize 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Power Institute, University of Sydney (Culture+)

Location: Australia (participating universities)

Award: National public speaking prize (award for the winning art talk on French and Australian cultural ties). No application fee.

Duration: Three-stage competition during 2026 (university shortlist, recorded talk, national live final)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual national public speaking challenge for students at participating Australian universities, rewarding the best art talk on French and Australian cultural ties. The 2026 theme is 'Art and Environment, France and Australia: Our forests and trees in danger': students compare two artists, one Australian and one French (at least one a visual artist), analysing how a sensibility to nature shaped their work on forests and trees from the 19th century to the present. THREE STAGES: (1) submit a 400-word summary paper outlining a proposed 7-minute talk; university panels shortlist three applicants; (2) shortlisted applicants submit a recording of the full 7-minute talk and panels select one representative per university; (3) representatives compete in a live (or recorded) national final. Stage 1 summaries are judged on compliance with instructions (10%), quality of sources (15%), relevance to theme (15%), writing and structure (20%), and interest/depth/originality (40%). ELIGIBILITY: students in Arts or Humanities disciplines; in the final year of a Bachelor degree, or Honours, Graduate Certificate, Masters or PhD students; enrolled for the first semester of the year at a participating institution; over 18. Stage 1 due 11:59PM, 31 May 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: workspacebrussels: Decoratelier Open Call 2026 (Spatial Research Residency)
item.description:
Organisation: workspacebrussels (residency at Decoratelier, Molenbeek)

Location: Decoratelier, Molenbeek, Brussels, Belgium

Award: EUR 5,000 budget (incl. VAT) for the residency, plus a 10x18m warehouse hall, wood and metal workshop access, reusable materials, junior-builder support three days a week, artistic and technical feedback, a possible internal showing, and weekday lunch. No application fee.

Duration: Three-week research residency (5 to 23 October 2026)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Research-focused residency at Decoratelier in Molenbeek, organised through workspacebrussels, for artists and collectives working with space, scenography, installation, architecture and performative environments. The residency prioritises research and material experimentation over production: applicants propose an investigation into spatial practice, ephemeral architecture or scenographic experimentation connected to the performing arts, engaging directly with the warehouse architecture and reusable materials. ELIGIBILITY: artists or collectives based in Belgium or abroad. NOTE: this is a context-specific research residency and does not provide scenographic support for existing productions. Apply via the workspacebrussels Decoratelier open call.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Artists' Futures Fund: Breakthrough Artist Fellowship 2026-27
item.description:
Organisation: Artists' Futures Fund

Location: England and Wales, UK (in-kind studio space at a partner institution)

Award: GBP 10,000 unrestricted bursary over the 10-month fellowship, plus in-kind studio space, mentorship from a partner institution, professional development, and a co-curated end-of-fellowship group showcase. No application fee.

Duration: 10 months (October 2026 to July 2027)

Deadline: 12 Jun 2026

Needs-based fellowship from the Artists' Futures Fund bridging the gap from graduation to professional visual-arts practice. ELIGIBILITY: recent BA or MA visual-arts graduates (within two years of October 2026) from an AFF partner institution (Cardiff School of Art & Design, Chichester College Group, Liverpool Hope, Loughborough, Manchester Metropolitan, City of Portsmouth College, Sunderland, Swansea College of Art UWTSD), or a relevant Level 3 UAL Diploma/A Levels from City of Portsmouth College; must be eligible to live and work in England or Wales, facing socio-economic, mental and/or physical health barriers, and able to commit fully to the 10 months without additional formal study. Applications open 15 May 2026. Apply via the Artists' Futures Fund support page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Art Zoyd Studios: Electroacoustic & Electronic Music Residency 2026/2027
item.description:
Organisation: Art Zoyd Studios

Location: Valenciennes, France (in-person residency)

Award: Funded residency: accommodation and meals covered during the residency, travel reimbursed up to EUR 400, and support from a computer-music designer; a copyright bonus is possible. No artist fee or stipend. No application fee.

Duration: Residency across the 2026/2027 season

Deadline: 29 May 2026

Production residency at Art Zoyd Studios in Valenciennes for composers, musicians and sound artists working in electroacoustic and electronic music, with technical support from a computer-music designer to develop new work. ELIGIBILITY: composers, musicians and artists practising in electroacoustic or electronic music; open worldwide. Apply via the Art Zoyd Studios residency request page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence 2026 (Oak Spring)
item.description:
Organisation: Oak Spring Garden Foundation

Location: Upperville, Virginia, USA (2-5 week on-site residency)

Award: $10,000 grant plus a two- to five-week residency with accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Two- to five-week residency

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Eliza Moore Fellowship for Artistic Excellence from the Oak Spring Garden Foundation, supporting early-career artists whose work engages plants, gardens and landscapes. ELIGIBILITY: early-career visual and literary artists, dancers and musicians; no nationality restriction stated, though the residency takes place on site in Virginia. Apply via the Oak Spring fellowships page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Diriyah Art Futures: Emerging New Media Artists Program 2026/27 (Riyadh)
item.description:
Organisation: Diriyah Art Futures (DAF), in collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains

Location: Diriyah, Saudi Arabia (in-person, intensive on-site programme)

Award: Production budget; access to advanced audio-visual labs, immersive-reality and spatial audio-visual environments; cutting-edge professional equipment; multidisciplinary mentorship from international digital and new-media artists; and a culminating curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. Specific stipend amount not stated in the public call. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately one year (programme starts October 2026), with three months of thematic and conceptual seminars followed by a practice-based phase and a final group exhibition in October 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Third cycle of Diriyah Art Futures' Emerging New Media Artists Program, a developmental collaboration with Le Fresnoy - Studio National des Arts Contemporains. The first three months centre on thematic and conceptual exploration through seminars, lectures, screenings and workshops spanning digital technology, socio-cultural dynamics, the environment, more-than-human life and cultural heritage. The programme then moves into a practice-based phase with personalised advisory and access to DAF's labs, encompassing advanced audio-visual techniques, immersive reality and spatial audio-visual environments, concluding with a curated group exhibition at DAF in October 2027. ELIGIBILITY: emerging creative practitioners under 35, at graduate or postgraduate level, with experience in modern media and digital art; open worldwide with a particular focus on practitioners from the Middle East and North Africa. Apply via the linked Ministry of Culture engagement portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS): International Creator Residency 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS), Tokyo Metropolitan Foundation for History and Culture

Location: TOKAS Residency, 2-14-7 Tatekawa, Sumida-ku, Tokyo, Japan (single room 25 sqm; twin room 49 sqm for duos; shared studios 125 sqm and 48 sqm)

Award: Round-trip economy-class airfare between nearest airport and Tokyo (Narita or Haneda); per diem living expenses of JPY 4,200 per day (excl. tax); fee for work/project of JPY 300,000 (excl. tax); living space (single room) and access to shared studio. Note: 20.42% income tax is deducted from per-diem and project-fee amounts. For duos, airfare is provided for both members but per diem and project fee are paid for one person only. No application fee.

Duration: Three-month residency in one of three slots: May-July 2027, September-November 2027, or January-March 2028 (six creators total, two per slot), concluding with an Open Studio research presentation

Deadline: 16 Jun 2026

Tokyo Arts and Space (TOKAS) International Creator Residency invites practitioners to develop new works and ideas in Tokyo, with research outcomes presented at a closing Open Studio at TOKAS Residency. The programme fosters cross-cultural creative exchange across visual arts (drawing, installation, painting, performance, photography, media art, sculpture, sound art, video, etc.), design and architecture. ELIGIBILITY: creators residing outside Japan with at least five years of experience in visual arts, design or architecture; sufficient English to communicate with residents and TOKAS staff; independent and capable of working and living on one's own; duos eligible if at least one member resides outside Japan and they have completed at least one prior project together; students ineligible except PhD candidates. CONDITIONS: must stay consecutively at TOKAS Residency for the proposed period; create work for the Open Studio and submit a comprehensive project report; cooperate with TOKAS public relations; no commercial or for-profit activities; accommodation is for the participating creator only (no family/partners/friends/guests). Apply via the TOKAS online submission form by 16 June 2026, 18:00 JST: application form (PDF, in English), two letters of recommendation from referees at art and cultural institutions, and a portfolio (max 6 A4 landscape pages, max 10MB, up to 3 projects).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: BC Arts Council: Individual Arts Grants - Media Artists 2026
item.description:
Organisation: BC Arts Council (Province of British Columbia)

Location: British Columbia, Canada (provincial council; BC residency typically required)

Award: Up to $25,000 CAD per project. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 17 Jun 2026

BC Arts Council's Individual Arts Grants stream for independent media artists working in moving image (film, video, narrative, experimental, expanded cinema, installation), audio/sound art (sound sculptures, installations, sound walks, gallery presentations) or new media and digital arts (interactive installations, immersive environments, web-based art, ICT). ELIGIBILITY: independent individual media artists (not groups or organisations); projects must be independent of commercial industries. As a provincial arts council, BC Arts Council typically requires applicants to be residents of British Columbia - confirm current residency requirements on the linked page before applying. NOTE: registration must be completed by 17 June 2026 at 23:59 PT, ahead of the 24 June application deadline. Applications via the SmartSimple Grant Management System. Contact: Paneet Singh, Program Advisor (Paneet.Singh@gov.bc.ca).

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: The Lab: Living Wage Residencies 2026-27 (San Francisco)
item.description:
Organisation: The Lab

Location: San Francisco Bay Area, California, USA (eligibility restricted to artists based in the Bay Area; significant in-person access to The Lab's space in the Mission District is required)

Award: USD 17,530 artist fee per resident (commensurate with three months of work at a San Francisco Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator) plus USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials. Three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space, plus two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces for the residency outcome. Curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. Stage-2 finalists receive an additional USD 250 stipend to develop their full proposal. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies take place between July 2026 and June 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

The Lab's flagship Living Wage Residency, a substantial in-person residency in San Francisco's Mission District for artists working in any media and discipline. Since 2014 the programme has directed over USD 1 million to artists, with artist fees representing nearly 30% of The Lab's average operating budget. The Lab's history is as a site for experimental and interdisciplinary work spanning performance, exhibition, installation, sound and experimental music, writing, institutional critique, social practice, parades, feasts and further disciplines yet to be defined. The Lab's mission centres traditionally underrepresented artists, particularly those identifying as BIPOC, LGBTQ+, women, disabled and/or from economically marginalised backgrounds. ELIGIBILITY: artists based in the San Francisco Bay Area (tight geographic restriction; in-person access to The Lab's space is central to the residency). SUPPORT per residency: USD 17,530 artist fee (3 months at a Bay Area living wage per MIT's Living Wage Calculator); USD 5,000 for collaborators and materials; three months of rehearsal/workshop access to The Lab's space (as available around other programming); two weeks of continuous use of the exhibition and performance spaces; curatorial and marketing support, outside studio visits, documentation and publicity. PROCESS: Stage 1 application (CV, 3 work links, brief project description and resource plan) due 31 May 2026; Stage 2 invites up to 12 selected artists to develop a full proposal with a USD 250 stipend (Stage 2 deadline TBD). Reviewed by a diverse panel of professional artists and curators plus The Lab's Board. Outside funding additionally secured for a proposal is not factored into the decision, but The Lab may help identify further sources. Supported by the San Francisco Arts Commission.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stimuleringsfonds: Activity Programme Grant 2027-2028
item.description:
Organisation: Stimuleringsfonds Creatieve Industrie (Creative Industries Fund NL)

Location: Netherlands

Award: EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year for the 2027 and 2028 calendar years

Duration: Two years, 2027 and 2028

Deadline: 15 Jun 2026

Two-year activity-programme funding from the Creative Industries Fund NL for organisations active in design, digital culture or architecture. Supports a coherent multi-year programme of activities running across 2027 and 2028 (rather than single projects). The funding underwrites a portion of the organisation's annual programme budget, giving recipients planning stability over the two-year period. Application window 13 May - 15 June 2026. EUR 50,000 to EUR 125,000 per year per organisation, depending on the scale and ambition of the proposed programme. ELIGIBILITY: cultural and creative-industries organisations established in the Netherlands working in design, digital culture or architecture; a distinct scheme from the Fund's annual project grants, festival rounds and voucher schemes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stroom Den Haag: PRO Grants 2026 (Research / Projects / Programmes / Investment)
item.description:
Organisation: Stroom Den Haag

Location: The Hague, Netherlands (eligibility restricted to professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague)

Award: Four schemes - PRO Research, PRO Projects, PRO Programmes, and PRO Investment - with amounts typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+ depending on scheme and proposal.

Duration: Project-based

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Stroom Den Haag's PRO grants support professional visual artists registered with Stroom in The Hague through four schemes: PRO Research (research and development for new work), PRO Projects (project realisation), PRO Programmes (multi-stage artistic programmes spanning research, production and presentation), and PRO Investment (investment in artistic practice, e.g. equipment, studio, professional development). Three remaining 2026 application rounds: 31 May, 6 September and 25 October 2026. ELIGIBILITY: professional visual artists registered with Stroom in the municipality of The Hague (tightly geographic). Amounts vary by scheme and proposal, typically EUR 2,500 to EUR 20,000+.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: FILE NOT FOUND: Open Call for Archive Residencies
item.description:
Organisation: Goethe-Institut

Location: On-site at ten cultural archives around the world; final exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany

Award: EUR 4,000 (EUR 2,000 artist fee + EUR 2,000 production budget). Travel and accommodation covered up to EUR 2,000 for one person.

Duration: On-site residency of 2 weeks to 2 months, scheduled between August 2026 and May 2027

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Open call to participate in FILE NOT FOUND, a project exploring the risks and challenges archives face as sites of cultural memory, resistant knowledge and alternative perspectives under growing political pressure. Ten practitioners - artists, thinkers, activists, theatre makers, dancers, musicians and more - will be selected for residencies at ten cultural archives around the world. On site, residents engage with existing archival practices, explore both safe and not-so-safe spaces, investigate traditional and experimental methods of archiving, and ideally develop artistically subversive approaches that strengthen the resilience of archives against external influence. ELIGIBILITY: artists worldwide, all disciplines. Applicants review the ten participating archives and select the one they wish to apply to. The on-site residency runs 2 weeks to 2 months between August 2026 and May 2027. OUTCOME: an artwork plus a residency report, with a group exhibition at BLMK Cottbus, Germany in autumn 2027. Working language English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Passage Art-Residency-Programme 2026 (KOBRA Cultural Network)
item.description:
Organisation: Polygona Kunstverein e.V. (KOBRA Cultural Network)

Location: Wittenberg region, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany

Award: Per residency place: accommodation for the four-week stay; a grant of EUR 5,000; artists in residence with a child receive an additional EUR 1,000 toward temporary childcare. Travel, local transport, consumables and living expenses are paid by the artist; payment is made on receipt of an invoice.

Duration: Four weeks, October 2026

Deadline: 30 May 2026

Two residency places in the Passage Art-Residency-Programme run by Polygona Kunstverein e.V. as part of the KOBRA Cultural Network, which explores continuities and discontinuities in the cultural landscape between Flaeming and Duebener Heide in the district of Wittenberg (Saxony-Anhalt, Germany). National and international artists working in photography and film are invited to apply. The two 2026 residencies run in October 2026 in collaboration with KOBRA partners Ferropolis Foundation for Industrial Heritage and the Research Library of Reformation Studies Wittenberg (RFB). TOPIC ONE (Ferropolis): artistic perspectives on the history of the former Zschornewitz coal-fired power station, the transformation of industrial landscapes, and visions for the future of post-industrial areas. TOPIC TWO (RFB): exploring the research library as a store of knowledge and examining the production of knowledge and its visual representation, drawing on a collection of around 230,000 volumes including Reformation-era first editions, manuscripts and historical writings. ELIGIBILITY: minimum age 18, no age limit; students are not eligible. Artists are selected by jury on the quality of submitted work; selected artists notified from 20 June 2026. FUNDING per place: accommodation for the four-week stay, a EUR 5,000 grant, and an additional EUR 1,000 for childcare if attending with a child. OBLIGATIONS: four weeks present in October 2026, realisation of an artistic project, provision of ten project photographs by 31 December 2026, delivery of a one-day workshop for young people, a public artist talk, and two days participating in filming for a documentary. Artists retain copyright and ownership of works created. Childcare and child-friendly accommodation can be arranged; partners may also accompany the artist at the artist's own additional cost. No application fee. Apply via the online form at passageair.org by 30 May 2026; applications accepted in German or English.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Gasworks Residency Open Call 2026 (Artists based in Greece)
item.description:
Organisation: Gasworks

Location: London, United Kingdom (residency held at Gasworks; applicants must be based in Greece)

Award: Fully funded residency: single-room accommodation in Gasworks' Residency House (shared two-storey house with three communal bathrooms and a kitchen); economy return flights and taxi transfers from the artist's home country to London (one cabin + one hold bag); all visa costs and visa application support; weekly stipend of £175 for living expenses; materials budget up to £800; prepaid TFL zones 1+2 travel card for unlimited central London travel throughout the residency.

Duration: Eleven weeks, 30 September - 15 December 2026

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Eleven-week fully funded residency at Gasworks in London for an artist based in Greece at a pivotal moment in their career. Gasworks' residency programme supports professional development, cultural exchange, and artistic experimentation, hosting up to twenty residencies a year. Residencies are self-led, non-prescriptive and process-based: artists are expected to be self-motivated and to lead on the research and production of their own work, while Gasworks provides administrative, pastoral and curatorial support. Includes 24/7 access to a fully accessible private studio at Gasworks alongside resident artists and London-based studio holders. ELIGIBILITY: artist based in Greece, at a pivotal moment in their career.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Magnetic 5: Franco-UK Artist Residencies (2026 call)
item.description:
Organisation: Fluxus Art Projects

Location: France and United Kingdom (artists from a French region apply to the associated UK residency; artists from a UK nation apply to the associated French region)

Award: Threshold monthly fee of £2,100 / €2,500, plus accommodation, access to a workspace, curatorial mentoring and networking opportunities.

Duration: Two to three months (residency dates set per host)

Deadline: 02 Jun 2026

Magnetic is a joint Franco-UK initiative coordinated by Fluxus Art Projects, bringing together ten institutions for a programme of 2-3 month artist residencies: five in France (Capc, Bordeaux; Frac Grand Large, Dunkirk; Frac Bretagne, Rennes; Villa Arson, Nice; Betonsalon, Paris) and five in the UK (Wysing Arts Centre and Gasworks in England; Flax Art Studios, Northern Ireland; Aberystwyth Arts Centre, Wales; Cove Park, Scotland). Artists from each of the five French regions apply for the associated UK residency; artists from each UK nation apply for the associated French region. Societal and environmental issues are at the heart of the programme; the residencies are situated, with selected artists expected to work in resonance with each host's geographical, historical, societal and artistic context.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Banff Centre: Visual Arts Thematic Residency - Future Figurations (2027)
item.description:
Organisation: Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity

Location: Banff, Alberta, Canada (in-person residency on the Banff Centre campus in the Canadian Rockies)

Award: Paid program. Total before scholarships: CAD $9,484.55 per person. With the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals): CAD $4,384.73 per person. Canadian Indigenous Scholarship: 100% tuition + accommodation + meals (no out-of-pocket). Application fee: CAD $65 (individuals/groups), CAD $35 for Indigenous applicants. No artist stipend.

Duration: 5 weeks total (arrival 24 January 2027, program 25 January-26 February 2027, departure 27 February 2027)

Deadline: 03 Jun 2026

Visual Arts Thematic Residency at Banff Centre led by artist-faculty Rajni Perera and Marigold Santos. Drawing on ideas of speculative futures, science fiction, myth-making, and world-building, artists are invited to research, imagine and create possible selves and possible worlds. The program considers futurity as an anti-oppressive and restorative force, exploring new possibilities of selfhood and agency by decolonising ideas of the body. Open to international applicants aged 18+ with post-secondary formal training or equivalent experience, working in visual arts disciplines: ceramics, digital media, painting, drawing, printmaking, papermaking, photography, sculpture, installation. Individual artists or duos (no more than two collaborators). Priority given to artists who have not attended a Banff visual arts residency in the past three years. Residency includes single bedroom on-campus accommodation, meal plan (~$68/day credit equivalent), individual studio space with shared production facility access, faculty mentorship and workshops, and access to Banff Centre facilities (fitness centre, library, performances). IMPORTANT: this is a paid program, not a stipend-paying residency. Total before scholarships is CAD $9,484.55; the Banff Centre Arts Scholarship (covering 100% tuition + 25% accommodation/meals) brings the out-of-pocket cost to CAD $4,384.73. A Canadian Indigenous Scholarship covers tuition, accommodation and meals in full. There is also a non-refundable application fee (CAD $65 standard, $35 Indigenous). Apply via the Banff Centre online portal with materials uploaded through SlideRoom.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WIELS: Residency Programme 2027 (Brussels)
item.description:
Organisation: WIELS

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at WIELS contemporary art centre)

Award: Artistic recognition grant of €3,000 per selected artist (4 artists selected). Awarded as an artistic distinction without any production obligation or service in return. Under Belgian tax law, artistic prizes may be treated as miscellaneous income and may benefit from a partial tax exemption within certain limits (depends on the beneficiary's individual situation).

Duration: Six months in 2027, choose one block: January-June 2027 OR July-December 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Annual residency programme at WIELS (Brussels-based centre for contemporary art). This call is open EXCLUSIVELY to Belgian artists or artists who have been living and working in Belgium for at least three years, and who have contributed to the Brussels contemporary art scene during that period. An independent jury selects four artists, each awarded a €3,000 artistic recognition grant. The grant is a distinction supporting the quality of the artistic practice; there is no production requirement or contractual service obligation attached. Residency period is six months in 2027 (January-June or July-December block, applicant choice). Application deadline 31 May 2026. Application materials via online form: personal information; artistic approach (max 250 words); statement of intent describing projects, ideas and expectations for the residency (max 250 words); three professional references (name, email, phone, profession); and portfolio + CV via Option 1 (Dropbox/Google Drive link with up to 10 images/videos plus a numbered descriptive list with title, date, technique, dimensions and ~50-word description per item, plus CV) or Option 2 (PDF with up to 10 captioned images plus CV). Videos may also be hosted on YouTube/Vimeo (with password if needed); videos of still works are not accepted. All share links must be set to 'anyone with the link can view'; links requiring access requests will not be considered. Questions: residency@wiels.org (do not email about results, notifications will be sent). Free to apply. Note: this is a Belgium-restricted call and is not open to artists without a Belgian / 3+ years-in-Belgium connection.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Headlands Center for the Arts: Artist in Residence 2027 (AIR)
item.description:
Organisation: Headlands Center for the Arts

Location: Sausalito, California, United States (Marin Headlands, Golden Gate National Recreation Area)

Award: Fully sponsored residency: studio space, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and living-expense stipend. No application fee.

Duration: 4 to 10 weeks, scheduled between January and December 2027

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Open call for the 2027 cohort of the Headlands Artist in Residence (AIR) program, one of the longer-running fully-sponsored residencies on the US West Coast. Approximately 50 local, national and international artists are selected each year for a residency of 4 to 10 weeks at the Headlands' Marin Headlands campus (former military buildings inside the Golden Gate National Recreation Area, just north of San Francisco). Residents receive a private studio, chef-prepared meals, on-site housing, travel costs, and a living-expense stipend, fully sponsored by the program. Open to artists at all career stages working in any medium: drawing, painting, sculpture, photography, film, video, new media, installation, fiction and nonfiction writing, poetry, dance, music, interdisciplinary practice, social practice, and architecture. Strong fit for new-media / interdisciplinary / research-driven practices. Application opens 1 April 2026 and closes 1 June 2026 via SlideRoom; applicants are notified of decisions by 11 December 2026. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Ohme x EXPERIENCE: Open Call - Experience Lab (Digital Artist Residency on Consciousness)
item.description:
Organisation: Ohme (in collaboration with EXPERIENCE / CRCN, Université libre de Bruxelles)

Location: Brussels, Belgium (in-person residency at Ohme's premises, USquare, Brussels)

Award: Artist fee: €5,300 (approx. 20 working days based on CP 304 salary scales, max 10 years experience). Production budget up to €2,000. Plus professional documentation, access to EXPERIENCE datasets/tools/documentation, preparatory reading list, Ohme team support, and workspace at USquare.

Duration: 20-day engagement spread between September and November 2026, with public restitution in December 2026. Preparatory period July-August 2026 (reading list and onboarding materials).

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Open call from Ohme (Brussels-based research and curatorial platform at the intersection of art and science) for a digital visual artist or collective to develop an artistic project in residence with EXPERIENCE, an ERC-funded research programme led by Prof. Axel Cleeremans at the Centre for Research in Cognition and Neurosciences (CRCN), Université libre de Bruxelles. EXPERIENCE investigates the nature of subjective experience (phenomenology) and builds 3D maps of 'phenomenal space' from large datasets of similarity and preference judgments. The selected artist works directly with these empirical datasets (similarity judgments, subjective preference data describing how people perceive, compare and value conscious experience) as primary creative material, not as illustration. Possible directions include information design that critically engages with visualising consciousness data; generative or algorithmic works using subjective valuation datasets as compositional grammar; data visualisation as artistic practice; creative coding exploring neural/latent space and perception/emotion/subjectivity; and critical or poetic approaches to measuring/mapping inner experience. Eligibility: visual artist or collective connected to the contemporary art field of the Fédération Wallonie-Bruxelles (live, work, or have a professional practice within the French-speaking art community of Belgium); established practice in digital art, new media, computational art, data visualisation or information design used as artistic practice; experience working with data as creative material (e.g. Processing, p5.js, openFrameworks, TouchDesigner, Max/MSP, or equivalent); critical engagement with the tools/systems behind information production and dissemination; interest in mind/perception/cognition/neuroscience; available in person in Brussels September-November 2026 and for the December restitution; English proficiency (working language); French and/or Dutch appreciated. Ohme is NOT looking for artists who already work on consciousness or neuroscience, but practitioners whose existing methods could be mobilised in this scientific context. Sample EXPERIENCE datasets: https://osf.io/suqkp/overview?view_only=fdc73f1485664a2c9c7571e0c62abe6a (paper: 'Exploring the role of micro-valence in the phenomenal space', https://doi.org/10.1093/nc/niag005). Selection by interdisciplinary jury of EXPERIENCE and Ohme representatives; interviews with top three candidates by phone/video/in-person in late June 2026; selected artist announced late June 2026. Application: single PDF in English to opencall@ohme.be (subject: 'Application Experience Lab') by 31 May 2026, 23:59 CET, including up-to-date CV (English), portfolio (English), 1-page motivation text (English) on interest in the residency and consciousness theme plus technical competences, and the completed contact form (downloadable from Ohme's site). Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Serpentine: Future Art Ecosystems R&D Fellowship - Art x Convergence (2026-2027)
item.description:
Organisation: Serpentine Arts Technologies (Future Art Ecosystems)

Location: London, United Kingdom (low-residency hybrid; three in-person weekend intensives in London required)

Award: £10,000 award per fellow (4 fellows: individuals or collectives), plus travel and accommodation support for the three London intensives, plus specialist seminars, workshops and structured peer learning through the cohort.

Duration: Six months, September 2026 to March 2027 (low-residency hybrid). Biweekly online cohort sessions and bespoke mentorship plus three in-person weekend intensives in London: 16-20 September 2026, 11-15 November 2026, and 24-28 March 2027.

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Inaugural Future Art Ecosystems (FAE) R&D Fellowship from Serpentine Arts Technologies, supporting individual practitioners and ecosystem development in art and advanced technologies. Four fellows (individuals or collectives) selected. Open to artists, curators, technologists, organisers and producers working at the intersection of art and advanced technologies. International applicants welcome provided they can attend the three in-person London intensives. Inaugural theme: Art x Convergence, framed as a prompt to explore how AI is reshaping cultural and societal systems and to develop new frameworks for embodiment, robotics, legal constructs, markets and planetary organisation in light of AI's capacity to pursue goals, model environments and act in the world. Fellows pursue a defined research question connected to their practice or a project in development, supported through professional and specialist mentorship, network development, cohort exchange, and public-facing process sharing. FAE was initiated by Victoria Ivanova in 2019; programme led by Tommie Introna, Tsige Tafesse, and Kay Watson. Info sessions online on 15 May and 22 May 2026. Application deadline midnight BST 7 June 2026. Free to apply.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pictoright Fonds: 2026 Project Funding Rounds
item.description:
Organisation: Pictoright Fonds

Location: Netherlands (NL-based visual makers)

Award: Up to €35,000 per project. Applications above €5,000 must show substantial co-financing from other sources.

Duration: Per project

Deadline: 07 Jun 2026

Pictoright's collective rights fund supports projects with a social, cultural or educational purpose serving a broad group of professional visual makers in design/illustration, photography, and fine art working/residing in the Netherlands. Six to seven rounds per year; remaining 2026 deadlines: 7 June, 23 August, 4 October, 8 November. Decisions communicated within ~6 weeks of each deadline. Maximum grant €35,000; for requests above €5,000 the project must be substantially co-financed by other sources. The remit is collective benefit (publications, infrastructure, sector-wide research, exhibitions, education, professional development for the field), not individual practice grants, so projects need to articulate how they serve other visual makers, not just the applicant. Worth tracking for any artist-organised research, publication, exhibition, training or open-source-toolkit project benefitting the Dutch visual-maker community.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Peckham Digital: Programme Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Peckham Digital

Location: Peckham, London, United Kingdom (in-person festival 22 to 25 October 2026)

Award: Paid: facilitators and speakers compensated at Artist Union England pay rates (contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding application; if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead)

Duration: Festival programme slot (22 to 25 October 2026)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Open call for the 5th edition of Peckham Digital, a festival celebrating creative computing. This call is for the PROGRAMME track: artists, creative technologists and creative coders to facilitate workshops, present talks, provide demos, or deliver performances (the separate Artwork Open Call covers exhibition pieces). Emerging applicants explicitly welcomed; over half of past Peckham Digital artists had this as their first paid professional exhibition. Selected facilitators/speakers paid at Artist Union England rates. Workshop facilitators are asked whether their software will be open-source. Application requires: type of contribution (demo/workshop/talk/performance/other), 200-word description (text or video), 250-word facilitator statement on experience, 200 words on professional development impact, sample images or video, technical requirements, and any access needs. Equal Opportunities form also requested. Important caveat: festival is contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding being confirmed; if their funding application is not successful the festival will not go ahead.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Peckham Digital: Artwork Open Call 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Peckham Digital

Location: Peckham, London, United Kingdom (in-person festival 22 to 25 October 2026)

Award: Paid: each selected artist receives a fee (contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding application; outcome expected early July 2026; if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead). Submission to the open call is free.

Duration: Festival exhibition slot (22 to 25 October 2026)

Deadline: 14 Jun 2026

Open call for the 5th edition of Peckham Digital, a festival celebrating creative computing in all its shapes and forms. This call is for the ARTWORK EXHIBITION track only (the separate Programme Open Call covers talks, workshops, demos, performances and films). Looking for artists, creative technologists, creative coders and performers to exhibit artworks. Emerging applicants and early-career creative technologists explicitly welcomed; over half of past Peckham Digital artists had this as their first paid professional exhibition. Submission to the open call is free; selected artists receive a fee. Important caveat: festival is contingent on Peckham Digital's own funding being confirmed (decision expected early July 2026); if funding is not received the festival will not go ahead. Read the Application Guidelines before submitting.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: falía* AIR 2026 Artist Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Associazione falía*

Location: Lozio, Valle Camonica, Northern Italy

Award: €70/week stipend (2 to 6 weeks) + shared accommodation and workspace + co-production support

Duration: 2 to 6 weeks (summer)

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Summer artist residency in the mountain village of Lozio for individuals and collectives to create site-specific artworks, with preference for Land Art, Street Art and participatory art. Residents receive a weekly stipend, shared accommodation, workspace, cultural activities and co-production support; selected artworks become donations to the Municipality.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Call for Proposals: Alex Adriaansens Residency 2026
item.description:
Organisation: V2_ Lab for the Unstable Media

Location: Rotterdam, Netherlands

Award: €7,500

Duration: 3 months (starts 1 September 2026)

Deadline: 01 Jun 2026

Residency honouring V2_ co-founder Alex Adriaansens, aimed at upcoming curators, festival/programme initiators, community organisers and cultural practitioners in media art, digital cultures and related fields who are starting their professional careers. Duos or teams may apply (sharing the grant). No thematic requirement; applicants choose their own research focus and have access to V2_'s extensive archive. Apply with a letter of motivation (max 2 pages), short CV (max 1 page) and a specific research question (max 1 page) via the linked Google form, or email v2@v2.nl if you do not have a Google account. A jury of V2_ team members decides. Results will be presented in Rotterdam at a jointly organised event (e.g. expert meeting), or via an essay, or another format proposed by the resident.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SIT-PLU Residencies 2027 (4 host institutions across NL, IT, ES)
item.description:
Organisation: SIT-PLU consortium: Baltan Laboratories (NL), Lungomare (IT), Idensitat (ES), ZEMOS98 (ES), with LUCA School of Arts (BE), Floating University (DE), EINA (ES), Universitat Politècnica de València (ES). Funded by Creative Europe.

Location: Choose one host context: Wondermash / Meuse Delta (NL), Bolzano riverscapes (IT), Besos river sacrifice zones (ES), Galicia energy transition (ES)

Award: €13,500 artist fee (travel excluded) + production budget (varies by host)

Duration: 1 year residency starting January 2027

Deadline: 31 May 2026

Situated Creative Practices for the Pluriverse (SIT-PLU) is a Creative Europe cooperation project tackling socio-ecological challenges through cross-disciplinary research and context-specific artistic interventions. Drawing on the Zapatista concept of the pluriverse ("a world where many worlds fit") and buen vivir, the project foregrounds communal interdependence and human/more-than-human relationships. Selected residents engage with one specific context for one year and develop new forms of creative intervention/cultural mediation. Four host contexts available for 2027: Baltan Laboratories with Wondermash (Meuse Delta, NL), Lungomare on Bolzano riverscapes (IT), Idensitat on sacrifice zones near the Besos river mouth (ES), ZEMOS98 on local sustainable energy-transition models in Galicia (ES). Two-phase application: Phase 1 deadline 31 May 2026 23:59 CET (motivation letter, CV, portfolio of up to 5 projects, draft proposal targeting one host) — results by mid-July; three shortlisted practitioners per host then develop a more specific proposal in dialogue with the host institution (Phase 2 deadline 14 August 2026, results end of September). Each host runs an online Q&A between 11 and 15 May 2026. Enquiries: sitplu.project@luca-arts.be.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
